rationales.hedonism: She is still reviewing mock papers tonight after a day of meetings and says she only wants to sit with her chai for "five minutes," showing pleasure deferred to work demands.
rationales.achievement: A "long day of lesson planning" plus committee meetings and more papers to review reflects sustained effort toward competent performance at work.
She frames the threatened change against "twenty-two years of building this department's board results," emphasizing pride in long-term performance and standards.
rationales.security: Her distress centers on uncertainty and loss of predictability, and she says, "I need to find out what's actually being proposed before I react."

rationales.achievement: She highlights the percentile results and says, "This is what it looks like when the work actually pays off," treating the outcome as evidence of effective effort and method.
rationales.benevolence: She has been "drilling him on wave mechanics every evening for two months," investing steady personal effort to help someone close succeed.

rationales.achievement: The entry focuses on parents asking about "what rank" students can get and on being thanked for "board results," with recognition for strong performance clearly mattering to her.

rationales.stimulation: She considers the Bangalore role but rejects it because "the timing is impossible" and wonders if she uses the children "as an excuse not to take chances," explicitly shrinking from change.
rationales.security: She turns away from a higher-paying move because it would mean "uprooting Arjun" and disrupt Priya's board year, prioritizing stability over opportunity.
rationales.benevolence: Her decision is anchored in protecting Arjun's and Priya's schooling rather than pursuing the better-paid role for herself.

rationales.self_direction: She says it felt right to be "using my experience to shape the outcome" and defines the wrong move as "sitting quiet and hoping someone else would fix it."
rationales.achievement: She draws on "Twenty-two years" of experience and actively contributes a concrete curriculum proposal, showing competence-directed engagement rather than passivity.
rationales.security: She explicitly says, "What bothered me last time was the uncertainty," and feels able to work with the plan once it is concrete and predictable.
rationales.benevolence: She speaks up so "students aren't disadvantaged when they specialize," centering the welfare of those she teaches.

rationales.security: As soon as voluntary retirement comes up, she "ran the numbers" and catalogs lost medical benefits and housing allowance, showing immediate focus on downside protection.
rationales.benevolence: She recognizes that he "probably just wanted to talk" but she "turned it into a risk assessment," missing the emotional support her spouse was seeking.

rationales.achievement: She is fixated on the pilot going well, the annual evaluation, and the prior "exceeds expectations" rating, all tied to measurable success and recognition.
rationales.power: She notes the role brings "more visibility with the administration" and that the principal asked her to lead work that shows "the department runs on my curriculum design."
rationales.benevolence: She admits Priya is "quietly doing her work" and that she "should probably check in with her more" but does not because everything else fills the days.

rationales.hedonism: She chooses to "put the papers down" and let "marking wait until morning," savoring an hour that leaves the house feeling "the most relaxed" it has in weeks.
rationales.benevolence: She interrupts her own work to spend an hour on Priya's project and encourages her future by saying she could "absolutely" pursue research someday.

rationales.self_direction: Even while telling herself the timing will not work, she reads the entire posting and "bookmarked the school's website," preserving a personally chosen possibility instead of fully shutting it down.

rationales.achievement: She assembles evidence like pass rates, pilot progress, and olympiad qualifiers and says, "I know how to build a case," showing active concern with demonstrated competence and recognition.
rationales.security: She stresses that "the increment matters" because fees, tutoring, and coaching "all add up," linking performance directly to household stability.
rationales.benevolence: When asked what is actually the point, she answers, "Taking care of them," making family support the clear end goal of her work.

rationales.power: The hardest part is that Noor had "already looked up flat prices" and had effectively "decided without me," showing that being left out of the decision is what stings most.
rationales.security: Her London plan leaves him unable to sleep, and his reaction is driven by threat to the familiar family arrangement rather than excitement about change.

rationales.tradition: He dwells on maamoul, the kitchen smelling like when Samira was alive, and Hana shaping it "exactly the way her mother did," treating inherited practice as deeply meaningful.
rationales.benevolence: The whole entry centers on spending time with Hana and Tariq and feeling sustained by their wish to keep doing this together every Friday.

rationales.self_direction: He rejects the idea of letting Noor go and frames keeping people close as preferable to allowing the independent move his brother is defending.
rationales.security: His defense is about preserving closeness and keeping the family unit physically near, rather than tolerating uncertainty or separation.
He finally names the fear as, "if she leaves, the rest follow. House falls apart," making stability and cohesion the threatened center of the entry.

rationales.tradition: The significance lies in Noor cooking mansaf "from scratch" and setting the platter "the way I always do," a clear signal that family practice is being carried forward.

rationales.self_direction: He reflects on having once wanted "to study abroad" and worries he is now "doing the same thing to her," which shows growing support for Noor making her own path.
rationales.power: He criticizes the pattern of not letting children leave and recognizes his own impulse to repeat that control as something he regrets rather than defends.
rationales.tradition: He emphasizes that Noor "learned all of it here, in this kitchen, from watching me," focusing on what is transmitted across generations even if she moves away.

rationales.security: The looming scholarship is painful because "the house will be so quiet without her," and saying yes feels too final, showing strong attachment to a stable home life.
rationales.tradition: The entry lingers on Eid preparations, shared food, and a full multigenerational house that felt "exactly right," grounding meaning in established family ritual.
rationales.benevolence: His ideal moment is everyone gathered together and Noor helping in the kitchen, reflecting deep fulfillment in caring for and being surrounded by close family.

rationales.self_direction: He tells Noor she "should accept the scholarship" and even says she should return only "when she wants to," explicitly backing her independent choice.
rationales.power: By telling her not to come back out of obligation, he gives up the claim to direct her movements for his own comfort.
rationales.benevolence: Despite the pain, he supports what is best for Noor and sits with her grief tenderly at the kitchen table rather than pulling her back.

rationales.self_direction: He actively helps compare rental listings and reflects that "you hold something tightly and then you open your hand," showing growing acceptance of Noor's independent move.
rationales.tradition: Checking which neighborhoods have "halal shops nearby" shows he wants her new life to stay connected to familiar cultural practice.
rationales.benevolence: He spends time helping Noor plan the move and agrees to the family group chat so the relationship can stay close across distance.
